ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON DEC-05-2012 IN 503, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF. HEARING HELD. THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IS GRANTED PER C.C.P. 36(A) AND 36(D). TRIAL IS 04/02/13 AT 11:15AM IN DEPT. 503. LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY MOTIONS IS MARCH 26. PLAINTIFF TO MAKE A SETTLEMENT DEMAND ON ALL DEFENDANTS NO LATER THAN MARCH 8. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IN ROOM 603 AT 1:30 P.M. ON 03/21/13. TIME TO RESPOND TO WRITTEN CONTENTION DISCOVERY IS SHORTENED TO 20 DAYS. FACT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS MARCH 15. EXPERT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF IS MARCH 29. NO LATER THAN 12/28/12, PLAINTIFF MUST PROVIDE A STATEMENT STATING, AS TO EACH DEFENDANT, THE NAME AND CONTACT INFORMATION OF EACH PERSON WITH KNOWLEDGE OF ANY FACTS SUPPORTING ANY OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. NO LATER THAN 12/28/12, DEFENDANTS MUST PROVIDE A STATEMENT STATING THE NAME AND CONTACT INFORMATION OF EACH PERSON WITH KNOWLEDGE OF ANY FACTS SUPPORTING ANY OF DEFENDANT'S AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. ANY PERSON NOT IDENTIFIED ON THE STATEMENTS WILL BE BARRED FROM TESTIFYING AT TRIAL OR PRESENTING A DECLARATION ON A SUMMARY MOTION ABSENT GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON 03/13/13 AT 1:30PM IN DEPT. 503. A FURTHER SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE WITH JUDGE JACKSON WILL BE HELD ON 03/26/13 AT 1:30 P.M. THE PARTIES SHALL COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 5.0. ALL COUNSEL WITH THE REMAINING PARTIES / DECISION MAKERS HAVE TO BE PRESENT WITH AUTHORITIES TO SETTLE UNLESS THE COURT DETERMINES, PRIOR TO THE SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE, THAT GOOD CAUSE EXISTS TO HAVE THAT PERSON/PERSONS APPEAR BY TELEPHONE. ALL PARTIES ARE REQUIRED TO SUBMIT CONFIDENTIAL SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE STATEMENTS TO THE COURT. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS TO PREPARE AN ORDER AND FORWARD TO BERRY & BERRY FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: PATRICIA RODRIGUEZ OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436

ASBESTOS DISCOVERY 503, PLAINTIFFS JAMES SHIFFER & ESTHER SHIFFER'S MOTION TO HAVE PLAINTIFFS' REQUESTS DEEMED ADMITTED, TO STRIKE GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY'S OBJECTIONS, AND FOR SANCTIONS. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO HAVE PLAINTIFFS' REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION DEEMED ADMITTED AND TO STRIKE DEFENDANT GENERAL ELECTRIC'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFFS' WRITTEN DISCOVERY IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THEY SUFFICIENTLY TRIED TO RESOLVE THIS ISSUE WITHOUT COURT INTERVENTION AS REQUIRED BY THE PARTIES' STIPULATED ORDER. FURTHER, THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION WILL NOT BE DEEMED ADMITTED BECAUSE DEFENDANT SERVED ITS VERIFICATIONS PRIOR TO THE HEARING ON THIS MOTION. (C.C.P. 2033.280.) DEFENDANT DID NOT WAIVE ITS OBJECTIONS BECAUSE IT SERVED TIMELY OBJECTIONS AND NO VERIFICATIONS ARE REQUIRED FOR OBJECTIONS. (C.C.P. 2030.250, 2031.250, 2033.240; FOOD 4 LESS SUPERMARKETS, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1995) 40 CAL.APP.4TH 651.) PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: ERIN F. ROBINSON OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 12199

ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON MAY-23-2013 IN 503, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER MAINTAINING CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO MAINTAIN THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36(A), (D) IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF C.C.P. 36(A) BECAUSE THEY FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT MR. SHIFFER'S HEALTH IS SUCH THAT PREFERENCE IS NECESSARY TO PREVENT PREJUDICING HIS INTEREST IN THE LITIGATION. PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF C.C.P. 36(D) BECAUSE THEY FAILED TO SUBMIT CLEAR AND CONVINCING MEDICAL DOCUMENTATION RAISING SUBSTANTIAL MEDICAL DOUBT OF MR. SHIFFER'S SURVIVAL BEYOND SIX MONTHS. WHEN READ IN CONJUNCTION WITH HIS PROGRESS NOTES AND DEPOSITION TESTIMONY, DR. JAHAN'S DECLARATION IS INSUFFICIENT. Hearing held. The Court took Defendant General Electric Company's motion to vacate the trial date under submission. Each side submitted a proposed order for the Court's consideration. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kyle McLean of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13787 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Defendant Cbs Corporation'S (Fka Viacom Inc., Fka Westinghouse Electric Corporation) Memorandum Of Costs
Off Calendar Oct-22-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Plaintiffs' motion to tax defendant CBS Corp.'s memorandum of costs.

APPEARANCES: Oren P. Noah, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP 898-1555 for pltfs & moving parties; Mary Katherine Back, Esq. of Pond North LLP 217-1240 for deft & opposing party CBS Corporation.

RULING: Defendant sought clarification of the final amount of costs to be awarded and to have that amount included in the order. Defendant's calculations arrived at the amount of $19,511.98. Plaintiffs did not object, therefore the Court includes this amount in the order and adopts its remaining tentative ruling: Plaintiffs' motion to tax defendant CBS Corp.'s memorandum of costs is granted in part and denied in part. Defendant's memorandum of costs is taxed $1144 for costs related to pro hac vice admissions because defendant failed to demonstrate that such costs were reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient or beneficial to its preparation. Defendant's memorandum of costs is taxed $9,653.50 for costs identified as "Custodian of Records for PG&E" because it is either disallowed under C.C.P. 1033.5(b) or even if not explicitly disallowed defendant failed to demonstrate that such cost was reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient or beneficial to its preparation. Plaintiffs' motion is denied as to the RecordTrak charges as such charges are allowed and were incurred, reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient or beneficial to its preparation, and reasonable in amount. Plaintiffs have withdrawn their motion as to all other items. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kimberly R. Hendershott of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12552 (877/451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Off Calendar May-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties James Shiffer and Esther Shiffer
Charles T. Sheldon of WALSWORTH FRANKLIN BEVINS & McCALL, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant General Electric Company
Jon C. James of HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP (415-288-9800), appearing for defendant Sequoia Ventures Inc.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER MAINTAINING CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE

No objection made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to maintain the current trial date with preference pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a), (d) is denied without prejudice. Plaintiffs failed to meet the requirements of C.C.P. 36(a) because they failed to demonstrate that Mr. Shiffer's health is such that preference is necessary to prevent prejudicing his interest in the litigation. Plaintiffs failed to meet the requirements of C.C.P. 36(d) because they failed to submit clear and convincing medical documentation raising substantial medical doubt of Mr. Shiffer's survival beyond six months. When read in conjunction with his progress notes and deposition testimony, Dr. Jahan's declaration is insufficient. The court however will maintain the trial date and will treat it as an expedited trial. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Dana Freed of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #10602 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Off Calendar Mar-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Emily D. Bergstrom of BECHERER KANNETT & SCHWEITZER (510-658-3600), appearing for defendant / moving party Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties James Shiffer and Esther Shiffer

RULING:
DEFENDANT DILLINGHAM CONSTRUCTION N.A., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Dillingham Construction, N.A., Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. sections 437c(a) and 1010.6 and CRC 2.251. Defendant's proof of service shows that defendant electronically served its motion on January 10, 2013. With a noticed hearing date of March 26, 2013, defendant failed to provide proper notice by failing to extend the notice period by two court days as required by C.C.P. 1010.6. This court's order granting preference did not shorten such notice period nor did it state that e-service is the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiffs' objection based on improper notice is sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Derek S. Johnson of WALSWORTH FRANKLIN BEVINS & McCALL, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / opposing party General Electric Company

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS JAMES SHIFFER & ESTHER SHIFFER'S MOTION TO HAVE PLAINTIFFS' REQUESTS DEEMED ADMITTED, TO STRIKE GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY'S OBJECTIONS, AND FOR SANCTIONS

No argument was made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to have plaintiffs' requests for admission deemed admitted and to strike defendant General Electric's objections to plaintiffs' written discovery is denied. Plaintiffs failed to demonstrate they sufficiently tried to resolve this issue without court intervention as required by the parties' stipulated order. Further, the requests for admission will not be deemed admitted because defendant served its verifications prior to the hearing on this motion. (C.C.P. 2033.280.) Defendant did not waive its objections because it served timely objections and no verifications are required for objections. (C.C.P. 2030.250, 2031.250, 2033.240; Food 4 Less Supermarkets, Inc. v. Superior Court (1995) 40 Cal.App.4th 651.) Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. Prevailing party to prepare an order. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Erin F. Robinson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 12199 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Lauren N. Peebles of BERRY & BERRY (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel
(Refer to Sign-In Sheet for all other appearances.)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held. The Court ruled as follows: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted per C.C.P. 36(a) and 36(d). Trial is April 2, 2013 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day for hearing summary motions is March 26. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than March 8. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30 p.m. on March 21, 2013. Time to respond to written contention discovery is shortened to 20 days. Fact discovery cut-off date is March 15. Expert discovery cut-off is March 29. No later than Dec. 28, 2012, plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than Dec. 28, 2012, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Case management conference on March 13, 2013 at 1:30pm in Dept. 503. A further settlement conference with Judge Jackson will be held on March 26, 2013 at 1:30 p.m. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0. All counsel with the remaining parties / decision makers have to be present with authorities to settle unless the Court determines, prior to the settlement conference, that good cause exists to have that person/persons appear by telephone. All parties are required to submit confidential settlement conference statements to the Court. Counsel for plaintiffs to prepare an order and forward to Berry & Berry for approval as to form.
